old_claim.note: Scientific Instrument Making Guilds is contextual infrastructure or shared knowledge, not a strict hard prerequisite.

new_claim.note: Renaissance perspective stage systems depended on linear-perspective illusion to create deep scenic streets and controlled audience sightlines.

source_shape.source_locator: Smarthistory describes Teatro Olimpico as putting contemporary developments like linear perspective into practice and highlights Scamozzi's permanent forced-perspective stage set.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source connects Renaissance theater design to linear perspective and the forced-perspective stage set at Teatro Olimpico.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The corrected node centers on perspective stage systems, so perspective_in_art supplies the relevant visual method; scientific instrument guilds are not shown as a direct prerequisite.

ontology_before: Mechanical Theater Machinery depended on scientific_instrument_making_guilds through a weak context edge with no source-backed theater relationship.
ontology_after: Renaissance Perspective Stage Machinery depends on perspective_in_art for the visual method behind deep scenic streets and controlled sightlines.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: mechanical_theater_machinery now depends on perspective_in_art and no longer directly depends on scientific_instrument_making_guilds.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to scientific instruments used in theaters rather than stage scenery and machinery.
A source showed Teatro Olimpico-style stage systems did not use linear or forced perspective.
A narrower perspective-stagecraft node were introduced and should receive this edge instead.
short_reason: The sourced Renaissance stage system depends on perspective illusion, not scientific instrument guild organization.
